research.summary: Asaya is an active Indian D2C skincare brand by Wellspring Consumer Private Limited, positioned as melanin-first skincare for Indian and melanin-rich skin. Its range focuses on hyperpigmentation, dark spots, acne marks, body pigmentation, dehydration, dullness and sun protection, using science-led but emotionally affirmative language that rejects fairness-coded beauty ideals.
research.essence: For skin that was never the problem — only under-studied.
research.voice: Warm, affirmative, science-aware, intimate and reformist; an expert-friend tone that combines ingredient credibility, pigmentation education and melanin-positive reassurance with benefit-led D2C clarity.

palette.role: cocoa brown melanin and earth tone

research.aesthetic: Warm melanin-focused D2C skincare photography: products shot on smooth cream, lavender, peach, terracotta, beige and cocoa-toned studio surfaces with minimal environmental context; warm low-contrast colour grade; soft diffused daylight or large-source studio lighting from the front/side creating gentle shadows and luminous skin highlights; moderate styling density with texture smears, serum drops, sunscreen swatches, ingredient cues and tonal props rather than clutter; mobile-first close crops of tubes, jars, mists and roll-ons paired with benefit copy; frequent model imagery using Indian medium, dusky, brown and deep skin tones, showing face, shoulders, underarms, knees, elbows, neck and other pigmentation zones with real skin texture; mix of clean product-only ecommerce frames, product-in-hand demonstrations, before-after/progress-style social frames and educational tiles.

photoRules.grade: Use warm cream, lavender, peach, terracotta, beige, and cocoa-toned surfaces with diffused daylight or soft studio lighting. Show a spectrum of Indian skin tones, especially medium, dusky, brown, and deep complexions, with real skin texture, close-ups of application, body-zone crops, product textures, swatches, ingredient cues, before-after storytelling, and mobile-first product-plus-benefit compositions. Keep the grade warm, luminous, low-contrast, and healthy rather than blue-clinical or over-retouched.
signatures: Use warm cream, lavender, peach, terracotta, beige, and cocoa-toned surfaces with diffused daylight or soft studio lighting. Show a spectrum of Indian skin tones, especially medium, dusky, brown, and deep complexions, with real skin texture, close-ups of application, body-zone crops, product textures, swatches, ingredient cues, before-after storytelling, and mobile-first product-plus-benefit compositions. Keep the grade warm, luminous, low-contrast, and healthy rather than blue-clinical or over-retouched.

a concentric spotlight halo behind the subject

intelligence.overview: Asaya is an operating Indian D2C skincare brand at worldofasaya.com, owned or managed by Wellspring Consumer Private Limited. The brand positions itself as melanin-first skincare for Indian and melanin-rich skin, with a strong focus on hyperpigmentation, acne marks, sun protection, uneven tone, dehydration, and body pigmentation zones.
intelligence.purpose: To move Indian and melanin-rich skin from the margins of skincare formulation to the centre of product design, research, and beauty language.
intelligence.mission: To create researched, dermatologically and clinically tested skincare for melanin-rich Indian skin, especially for concerns such as hyperpigmentation, acne marks, moisture retention, sebum imbalance, sun exposure, and uneven tone.
intelligence.vision: To make melanin-rich skin the default, not the exception, in skincare research, product design, and beauty standards.
intelligence.story: Asaya’s origin story is rooted in co-founder Eeti Sharma’s experience with adult acne in her mid-30s and her frustration that expert counsel and European skincare products did not adequately address her dusky, melanin-rich skin. That gap revealed a larger formulation bias: Indian skin and melanin-rich skin had been under-studied, under-served, and often spoken to through Western beauty ideals. Asaya was launched by Eeti Sharma, Neeraj Biyani, Vikash Singhal, and Mandeep Bhatia to build skincare for skin like theirs, with gentler nourishing actives, stronger sun protection, deeper moisturisation, and a more respectful beauty language.
intelligence.positioning: Science-led skincare for Indian melanin-rich skin, built to treat pigmentation, protect tone, and celebrate every shade without chasing fairness.
intelligence.audience: Indian and melanin-rich skincare consumers who feel mainstream skincare has not been designed around their skin’s behaviour, especially people dealing with dark spots, post-acne marks, tanning, hyperpigmentation, uneven tone, dullness, dehydration, oily or acne-prone skin, sun exposure, and pigmentation in body areas such as underarms, knees, elbows, neck, inner thighs, and bum.
intelligence.persona: Rhea is a 27-year-old urban Indian consumer in Bengaluru, Mumbai, Delhi-NCR, Pune, or Hyderabad with medium-deep skin that tans easily and develops post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation after acne. She has tried Korean skincare, dermatologist prescriptions, viral Instagram products, and ingredient-led actives, but is tired of brightening claims that feel like fairness marketing. She wants visible results for acne marks, underarm darkness, friction pigmentation, SPF white cast, and uneven tone without being made to feel that her natural skin colour is the problem.
intelligence.toneOfVoice: Warm, affirmative, science-aware, intimate, reformist, and benefit-led.

palette.role: cocoa brown melanin and earth tone

typography.display: Soft, declarative modern sans-serif headlines with occasional editorial emphasis and all-caps moments.
typography.text: Clean, legible, mobile-first sans-serif UI typography suited to ecommerce, education, and ingredient communication.
typography.note: The typographic feel is contemporary D2C beauty rather than ornate luxury or stark pharma minimalism.

intelligence.logoSystem: The logo system appears to be word-led, centred on ASAYA and World of Asaya. The name carries the symbolic load with soft vowels, a sense of world-building, and a consumer-facing identity that is warmer than a purely clinical skincare house.
intelligence.photographyStyle: Use warm cream, lavender, peach, terracotta, beige, and cocoa-toned surfaces with diffused daylight or soft studio lighting. Show a spectrum of Indian skin tones, especially medium, dusky, brown, and deep complexions, with real skin texture, close-ups of application, body-zone crops, product textures, swatches, ingredient cues, before-after storytelling, and mobile-first product-plus-benefit compositions. Keep the grade warm, luminous, low-contrast, and healthy rather than blue-clinical or over-retouched.
intelligence.packagingStyle: Contemporary Indian D2C skincare packaging across tubes, bottles, mists, roll-ons, jars, serums, sunscreens, cleansers, and creams. Product naming mixes functional clarity, benefit shorthand, emotional naming, and proprietary science, with examples such as Advanced Spot Targeting Serum, Even Tone Underarm Mist, Fade Forward Inner Thigh & Bum Roll-On, Tone Restore Knee-Neck-Elbow Cream, Spot Light Sunscreen, Even Evermore Crème, Moisture Cocoon Crème, and MelaMe™.
intelligence.visualIdentity: Asaya’s visual world combines melanin-coded warmth, soft clinical beauty, and mobile-first D2C clarity. The system uses warm creams, lavender, peach, cocoa, plum, clean product cards, benefit-led education, and real Indian skin imagery to feel both scientifically credible and emotionally affirming.

competitors.name: Minimalist
competitors.note: India’s ingredient-led active skincare benchmark with transparent percentages and strong serum credibility; Asaya competes on actives but differentiates through melanin-first positioning and body pigmentation specificity.

competitors.name: The Derma Co.
competitors.note: Dermatology-coded performance skincare with strong digital marketing across acne, pigmentation, sunscreen, and actives; Asaya is warmer, more identity-led, and more specific to Indian melanin-rich skin.

competitors.name: Deconstruct
competitors.note: Ingredient-first Indian skincare brand with clean formulations and concern-led products; Asaya shares the educated active-skincare audience but has a more emotional and cultural platform.

competitors.name: Dot & Key
competitors.note: Bright, sensorial, social-friendly skincare brand competing on glow, sunscreen, pigmentation, and accessible pricing; Asaya’s edge is deeper pigmentation ownership and melanin-first science.

competitors.name: Foxtale
competitors.note: Social-first Indian skincare brand competing in active-led routines, sunscreens, and concern-based ecommerce.

competitors.name: Dr. Sheth’s
competitors.note: Indian-skin-focused skincare competitor with dermatologist-adjacent credibility and strong relevance in sunscreen, pigmentation, and barrier-care conversations.

social.note: Primary cultural and performance channel; likely high-frequency reels, UGC, influencer demonstrations, before-after narratives, pigmentation education, sunscreen try-ons, texture shots, founder or science storytelling, and conversion-led offers.
Education-suited channel for melanin science, hyperpigmentation routines, dermatologist-style explainers, product usage, and founder-led storytelling.
Corporate and recruitment-oriented channel covering hiring, operations, brand-building, funding, and growth as part of Wellspring Consumer.
Official linked platform; likely a secondary amplification channel for campaign, product, and ecommerce content.
Officially linked from the site but appears secondary to Instagram-led beauty discovery.
Officially linked from the site but appears secondary to Instagram-led beauty discovery.

press.title: How Asaya Makes Space for Melanin-Rich Skincare
Asaya launches as new skincare brand for melanin-rich skin
Skincare brand Asaya raises Rs 28 crore in pre-Series A funding led by RPSG Capital

insights: Asaya’s strongest territory is pigmentation without shame: helping consumers treat marks, patches, tanning, PIH, friction pigmentation, and sun damage without implying darker skin needs correction.

Body pigmentation is the brand’s unfair advantage because underarms, inner thighs, bum, knees, elbows, and neck are high-anxiety, high-search, high-conversion concerns that few prestige or clinical brands address tastefully.
The MelaMe™ platform should be made more ownable through diagrams, ingredient logic, clinical testing summaries, melanin-pathway explainers, founder and R&D content, and dermatologist validation.

The brand must counter ad fatigue with radical proof: real user diaries, no-filter progress, clinical endpoints, transparent testing methodology, shade-inclusive sunscreen wear tests, and clear limits on what products can and cannot do.

The visual identity can evolve from D2C promotional skincare into a modern Indian skin authority through richer skin-tone photography, slower editorial campaigns, melanin science visuals, sun and shadow metaphors, and premium but accessible packaging codes.

The founder story gives Asaya authenticity, but the bigger opportunity is to become the shared language for millions of Indians navigating acne marks, tanning, pigmentation, white cast, and skincare advice that has historically confused colour with damage.
